metadata.description: Name of the Microsoft Sentinel (Log Analytics) workspace

resources.comments: RULE-01 | Cryptojacking: GPU/High-Compute VM Deployed by First-Time Identity | T1496 | High
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890')]

properties.displayName: Azure Cryptojacking - High-Compute VM Deployed by New Identity
properties.description: Detects deployment of GPU or high-compute VMs (NC/NV/ND/NP/HB/HC series) by identities with no history of such deployments in the last 14 days. Attackers compromise Azure credentials and immediately provision GPU VMs for cryptocurrency mining (T1496). FP mitigations applied: (1) 14-day behavioral baseline excludes established GPU deployers; (2) known IaC/CI-CD caller patterns excluded by name; (3) Azure platform IPs (168.63.x, 169.254.x) excluded; (4) requires ActivityStatus='Success' only — not 'Start' or 'Accept' which are pre-execution noise. NOT a duplicate of built-in 'Unusual number of resources created' because this is SKU-specific and identity-baseline-driven.
properties.query:
let GPUSKUs = dynamic(["Standard_NC", "Standard_NV", "Standard_ND", "Standard_NP", "Standard_HB", "Standard_HC"]);
let KnownAutoPatterns = dynamic(["terraform", "bicep", "pipeline", "github", "pulumi", "devops", "arm-deployment"]);
let PriorGPUDeployers = AzureActivity
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(14d) .. ago(6h))
    | where OperationNameValue has_any ("VIRTUALMACHINES/WRITE", "VIRTUALMACHINESCALESETS/WRITE")
    | where Properties has_any (GPUSKUs)
    | where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
    | distinct Caller;
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(6h)
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| where OperationNameValue has_any ("VIRTUALMACHINES/WRITE", "VIRTUALMACHINESCALESETS/WRITE")
| where Properties has_any (GPUSKUs)
| where not(tolower(Caller) has_any (KnownAutoPatterns))
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| where CallerIpAddress !startswith "168.63." and CallerIpAddress !startswith "169.254."
| where Caller !in (PriorGPUDeployers)
| summarize
    DeploymentCount = count(),
    DeployedResources = make_set(ResourceId, 10),
    SourceIPs = make_set(CallerIpAddress, 5),
    CallerIP = any(CallerIpAddress),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Caller, ResourceGroup, SubscriptionId
| extend
    AccountName = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[0]),
    AccountUPNSuffix = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[1])

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: Cryptojacking: GPU VM deployed by {{Caller}} from {{CallerIP}}
alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: Identity {{Caller}} deployed {{DeploymentCount}} high-compute VM(s) with no prior history. Resources: {{DeployedResources}}

resources.comments: RULE-02 | Diagnostic Settings Permanently Deleted Without Recreation | T1562.008 | High
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/b2c3d4e5-f6a7-8901-bcde-f12345678901')]

properties.displayName: Azure - Diagnostic Settings Permanently Deleted Without Recreation
properties.description: Detects deletion of Azure Diagnostic Settings that are NOT re-created within 30 minutes. Attackers delete diagnostic settings to blind monitoring/SIEM ingestion before further activity (T1562.008). FP mitigations: (1) IaC/pipeline caller patterns excluded by name — Terraform 'destroy+apply' cycles typically recreate within seconds so the 30-min re-creation window handles those; (2) Only 'Success' status events — 'Start' is pre-execution noise; (3) Requires deletion NOT followed by timely recreation — catches destructive deletions vs. IaC cycles. NOT a duplicate of built-in 'NRT Security Event log cleared' which targets Windows OS event log, not Azure Diagnostic Settings.
properties.query:
let ExcludedCallerPatterns = dynamic(["terraform", "bicep", "pipeline", "github", "pulumi", "arm-"]);
let Deletions = AzureActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
    | where OperationNameValue =~ "MICROSOFT.INSIGHTS/DIAGNOSTICSETTINGS/DELETE"
    | where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
    | where not(tolower(Caller) has_any (ExcludedCallerPatterns))
    | where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
    | project DeletionTime = TimeGenerated, Caller, ResourceId, CallerIpAddress, SubscriptionId, ResourceGroup;
let Recreations = AzureActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
    | where OperationNameValue =~ "MICROSOFT.INSIGHTS/DIAGNOSTICSETTINGS/WRITE"
    | where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
    | summarize EarliestRecreation = min(TimeGenerated) by ResourceId;
Deletions
| join kind=leftouter Recreations on ResourceId
| where isnull(EarliestRecreation) or EarliestRecreation > DeletionTime + 30m
| summarize
    DeletionCount = count(),
    DeletedSettings = make_set(ResourceId, 10),
    SourceIPs = make_set(CallerIpAddress, 5),
    CallerIP = any(CallerIpAddress),
    FirstDeletion = min(DeletionTime),
    LastDeletion = max(DeletionTime)
    by Caller, SubscriptionId, ResourceGroup
| where DeletionCount >= 1
| extend
    AccountName = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[0]),
    AccountUPNSuffix = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[1])

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: Diag Settings deleted by {{Caller}} — no recreation within 30 min
alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: {{Caller}} deleted {{DeletionCount}} diagnostic setting(s) without timely recreation. Affected: {{DeletedSettings}}

resources.comments: RULE-03 | Mass Privileged Role Assignments by a Single Identity | T1098.003 | High
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/c3d4e5f6-a7b8-9012-cdef-123456789012')]

properties.displayName: Azure - Mass Privileged Role Assignments by Single Identity
properties.description: Alerts when a single identity assigns 5 or more non-read-only role assignments within a 30-minute window. Post-compromise privilege escalation by groups like LAPSUS$ and Storm-0501 involves rapid bulk role assignment to maintain access (T1098.003). FP mitigations: (1) PIM service principal excluded by name pattern — PIM-initiated bulk activations appear as role assignments but via 'MS-PIM' or 'PrivilegedIdentityManagement' callers; (2) Pure read-only roles (Reader, Billing Reader GUIDs) excluded; (3) Azure platform IP filtered; (4) Threshold of 5+ in 30 min avoids single legitimate assignment noise; (5) 30-minute bin ensures the burst pattern is detected rather than spread assignments.
properties.query:
let ExcludedCallerPatterns = dynamic(["ms-pim", "privilegedidentity", "identitygovernance", "aadidentitygovernance"]);
let ReadOnlyRoleIds = dynamic(["acdd72a7-3385-48ef-bd42-f606fba81ae7", "fa23ad8b-c56e-40d8-ac0c-ce449e1d2c64", "72fafb9e-0641-4937-9268-a91bfd8191a3"]);
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where OperationNameValue =~ "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEASSIGNMENTS/WRITE"
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| where not(tolower(Caller) has_any (ExcludedCallerPatterns))
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| where CallerIpAddress !startswith "168.63."
| extend RoleDefId = tolower(tostring(parse_json(Properties).requestbody.properties.roleDefinitionId))
| where not(RoleDefId has_any (ReadOnlyRoleIds))
| summarize
    AssignmentCount = count(),
    DistinctRoles = dcount(RoleDefId),
    AssignedRoles = make_set(RoleDefId, 10),
    AffectedScopes = make_set(ResourceGroup, 10),
    SourceIPs = make_set(CallerIpAddress, 5),
    CallerIP = any(CallerIpAddress),
    FirstAssignment = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAssignment = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Caller, SubscriptionId, bin(TimeGenerated, 30m)
| where AssignmentCount >= 5
| extend
    AccountName = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[0]),
    AccountUPNSuffix = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[1])

alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: {{Caller}} assigned {{AssignmentCount}} non-read-only roles in 30 minutes. Role IDs: {{AssignedRoles}}

resources.comments: RULE-04 | Storage Account SAS Token Bulk Generation | T1550 | Medium
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/d4e5f6a7-b8c9-0123-defa-234567890123')]

properties.displayName: Azure - Storage Account SAS Token Bulk Generation
properties.description: Detects when a single identity generates 3 or more SAS tokens (ListAccountSAS/ListServiceSAS) within a 30-minute window. Attackers that gain Azure management access generate SAS tokens as an alternative authentication material (T1550) to exfiltrate storage data without leaving standard AAD sign-in traces. These exact operation names were observed in this workspace. FP mitigations: (1) known backup and monitoring SP name patterns excluded; (2) threshold of 3+ in 30 min avoids single-use app auth flows; (3) correlated across both AccountSAS and ServiceSAS operation types — legitimate single-SA access typically uses one type.
properties.query:
let ExcludedPatterns = dynamic(["backup", "monitor", "storagesync", "azurebackup", "recov"]);
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
| where OperationNameValue has_any ("LISTACCOUNTSAS/ACTION", "LISTSERVICESAS/ACTION")
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| where not(tolower(Caller) has_any (ExcludedPatterns))
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| where CallerIpAddress !startswith "168.63."
| summarize
    SASGenerationCount = count(),
    TargetAccounts = make_set(tostring(split(ResourceId, "/")[8]), 10),
    OperationTypes = make_set(OperationNameValue, 3),
    SourceIPs = make_set(CallerIpAddress, 5),
    CallerIP = any(CallerIpAddress),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Caller, SubscriptionId, bin(TimeGenerated, 30m)
| where SASGenerationCount >= 3
| join kind=leftouter (
    StorageBlobLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
    | where OperationName == "GetBlob" and StatusCode == "200"
    | summarize BlobReadCount = count(), TotalBytesRead = sum(ResponseBodySize) by RequesterObjectId
) on $left.Caller == $right.RequesterObjectId
| extend
    AccountName = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[0]),
    AccountUPNSuffix = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[1])

alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: {{Caller}} generated {{SASGenerationCount}} SAS tokens within 30 min from {{CallerIP}}

resources.comments: RULE-05 | Azure Automation Runbook Created by First-Time Caller | T1059.009 | High
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/e5f6a7b8-c9d0-1234-efab-345678901234')]

properties.displayName: Azure - Automation Runbook Created or Published by First-Time Caller
properties.description: Detects creation or publishing of Azure Automation runbooks by identities with no history of runbook operations in the last 14 days. Attackers create runbooks that execute code at scale with Automation Account managed identity privileges — a cloud-native persistence and execution vector (T1059.009). Seen in APT29 and LAPSUS$ TTPs. FP mitigations: (1) 14-day behavioral baseline excludes developers/automation owners already working with runbooks; (2) known DevOps pipeline caller name patterns excluded; (3) Azure platform IPs excluded; (4) only 'Success' status — draft saves ('Start') excluded.
properties.query:
let KnownRunbookCallers = AzureActivity
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(14d) .. ago(6h))
    | where OperationNameValue has_any ("AUTOMATION/AUTOMATIONACCOUNTS/RUNBOOKS/WRITE", "AUTOMATION/AUTOMATIONACCOUNTS/RUNBOOKS/PUBLISH/ACTION", "AUTOMATION/AUTOMATIONACCOUNTS/RUNBOOKS/DRAFT/WRITE")
    | where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
    | distinct Caller;
let ExcludedPatterns = dynamic(["automation-account", "devops", "pipeline", "github", "bicep", "terraform"]);
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(6h)
| where OperationNameValue has_any ("AUTOMATION/AUTOMATIONACCOUNTS/RUNBOOKS/WRITE", "AUTOMATION/AUTOMATIONACCOUNTS/RUNBOOKS/PUBLISH/ACTION")
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| where not(tolower(Caller) has_any (ExcludedPatterns))
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| where CallerIpAddress !startswith "168.63." and CallerIpAddress !startswith "169.254."
| where Caller !in (KnownRunbookCallers)
| summarize
    RunbookOpCount = count(),
    RunbookNames = make_set(tostring(split(ResourceId, "/")[-1]), 10),
    Operations = make_set(OperationNameValue, 5),
    SourceIPs = make_set(CallerIpAddress, 5),
    CallerIP = any(CallerIpAddress),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Caller, ResourceGroup, SubscriptionId
| extend
    AccountName = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[0]),
    AccountUPNSuffix = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[1])

alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: First-time runbook caller {{Caller}} performed {{RunbookOpCount}} runbook operation(s) on {{RunbookNames}}

resources.comments: RULE-06 | TI Indicator Match on Azure Management Plane Caller IP (STIX ThreatIntelIndicators) | Multiple TTPs | High
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/f6a7b8c9-d0e1-2345-fabc-456789012345')]

properties.displayName: Azure - Threat Intelligence Match on Management Plane Caller IP (STIX)
properties.description: Correlates CallerIpAddress from AzureActivity against active STIX 2.1 IP indicators from ThreatIntelIndicators. An IP match indicates adversary-controlled infrastructure calling Azure management APIs — characteristic of nation-state operators and ransomware affiliates managing compromised Azure tenants. Enriched with SigninLogs and AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs to confirm a preceding authenticated session from the same TI-matched IP, dramatically reducing false positives from NAT/proxy coincidences. FP mitigations: (1) Confidence >= 75 filters low-quality bulk TI; (2) IsActive == true, Revoked == false, IsDeleted == false, ValidUntil > now() filter stale/revoked indicators; (3) RFC 1918 and Azure platform IPs excluded; (4) Only IPv4 observables (ObservableKey =~ 'ipv4-addr:value'); (5) Only AzureActivity Success status; (6) SigninLogs/AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs corroboration within 2h window elevates confidence.
properties.query:
let LookbackWindow = 1d;
let PrivateRanges = dynamic(["10.", "192.168.", "172.16.", "172.17.", "172.18.", "172.19.", "172.20.", "172.21.", "172.22.", "172.23.", "172.24.", "172.25.", "172.26.", "172.27.", "172.28.", "172.29.", "172.30.", "172.31.", "127.", "169.254.", "168.63."]);
// --- STIX 2.1 IPv4 TI Indicators from ThreatIntelIndicators ---
let TIIPIndicators = ThreatIntelIndicators
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackWindow)
    | where IsActive == true
    | where Revoked == false
    | where IsDeleted == false
    | where ValidUntil > now()
    | where Confidence >= 75
    | where ObservableKey =~ "ipv4-addr:value"
    | where isnotempty(ObservableValue)
    | where not(ObservableValue has_any (PrivateRanges))
    | summarize
        ThreatType = any(tostring(Data.indicator_types)),
        TI_Description = any(tostring(Data.description)),
        ConfidenceScore = max(Confidence),
        ActivityGroupNames = any(Tags),
        TLPLevel = any(tostring(AdditionalFields.TLPLevel)),
        TI_Pattern = any(Pattern)
        by TI_IP = ObservableValue;
// --- Azure Management Plane events from TI-matched IPs ---
let MgmtPlaneHits = AzureActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackWindow)
    | where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
    | where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
    | where not(CallerIpAddress has_any (PrivateRanges))
    | join kind=inner TIIPIndicators on $left.CallerIpAddress == $right.TI_IP
    | project
        ActivityTime = TimeGenerated,
        Caller,
        CallerIpAddress,
        OperationNameValue,
        ResourceId,
        ResourceGroup,
        SubscriptionId,
        TI_IP,
        ThreatType,
        TI_Description,
        ConfidenceScore,
        ActivityGroupNames,
        TLPLevel,
        TI_Pattern;
// --- Corroborate with human sign-in from same TI IP (SigninLogs) ---
let UserSigninsFromTIIPs = SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackWindow)
    | where ResultType == "0"
    | where isnotempty(IPAddress)
    | where not(IPAddress has_any (PrivateRanges))
    | join kind=inner TIIPIndicators on $left.IPAddress == $right.TI_IP
    | project SigninTime = TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, SigninIP = IPAddress, UserDisplayName, AppDisplayName, RiskLevelAggregated;
// --- Corroborate with SP sign-in from same TI IP (AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs) ---
let SPSigninsFromTIIPs = AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackWindow)
    | where ResultType == "0"
    | where isnotempty(IPAddress)
    | where not(IPAddress has_any (PrivateRanges))
    | join kind=inner TIIPIndicators on $left.IPAddress == $right.TI_IP
    | project SPSigninTime = TimeGenerated, ServicePrincipalName, ServicePrincipalId, SPSigninIP = IPAddress, AppId;
// --- Aggregate management plane hits per caller ---
MgmtPlaneHits
| summarize
    OperationCount = count(),
    Operations = make_set(OperationNameValue, 10),
    SubscriptionIds = make_set(SubscriptionId, 5),
    ResourceGroups = make_set(ResourceGroup, 5),
    AffectedResources = make_set(ResourceId, 10),
    CallerIP = any(CallerIpAddress),
    ThreatType = any(ThreatType),
    TI_Description = any(TI_Description),
    ConfidenceScore = max(ConfidenceScore),
    ActivityGroupNames = any(ActivityGroupNames),
    TLPLevel = any(TLPLevel),
    FirstSeen = min(ActivityTime),
    LastSeen = max(ActivityTime)
    by Caller, TI_IP
// --- Left join to capture corroborating sign-ins (presence elevates confidence) ---
| join kind=leftouter (
    UserSigninsFromTIIPs
    | summarize UserSigninCount = count(), SigninUsers = make_set(UserPrincipalName, 5), MaxUserRisk = max(RiskLevelAggregated) by SigninIP
  ) on $left.TI_IP == $right.SigninIP
| join kind=leftouter (
    SPSigninsFromTIIPs
    | summarize SPSigninCount = count(), SigninSPs = make_set(ServicePrincipalName, 5) by SPSigninIP
  ) on $left.TI_IP == $right.SPSigninIP
| extend
    CorroboratedBySignin = isnotempty(UserSigninCount) or isnotempty(SPSigninCount),
    TotalSigninCount = coalesce(UserSigninCount, 0) + coalesce(SPSigninCount, 0),
    AccountName = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[0]),
    AccountUPNSuffix = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[1])
| project-away SigninIP, SPSigninIP

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: TI match (STIX): {{Caller}} called Azure MGMT from {{CallerIP}}, type: {{ThreatType}}
alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: Caller {{Caller}} performed {{OperationCount}} Azure management operations from TI-matched IP {{CallerIP}}.

resources.comments: RULE-07 | Azure Resource Lock or Policy Deleted | T1562 | Medium
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/a7b8c9d0-e1f2-3456-abcd-567890123456')]

properties.displayName: Azure - Resource Lock or Policy Assignment Deleted
properties.description: Detects deletion of Azure Resource Locks, Policy Assignments, or Policy Definitions — guardrail controls that prevent unauthorized resource actions. Attackers delete locks/policies before destructive operations (ransomware, crypto-jacking, data wiping) to avoid 'cannot delete locked resource' failures (T1562). FP mitigations: (1) Known IaC tool name patterns excluded — Terraform/Bicep legitimately destroy and re-create policies, but correlation with other rules (e.g., VM deploy) provides context; (2) Azure platform IPs excluded; (3) Only Succeeded operations; (4) Policy exemption WRITE is intentionally covered — attackers create exemptions to bypass policy without triggering a DELETE.
properties.query:
let HighImpactOps = dynamic([
    "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/LOCKS/DELETE",
    "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/POLICYASSIGNMENTS/DELETE",
    "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/POLICYDEFINITIONS/DELETE",
    "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/POLICYEXEMPTIONS/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/POLICYSETDEFINITIONS/DELETE",
    "MICROSOFT.MANAGEMENT/MANAGEMENTGROUPS/SUBSCRIPTIONS/DELETE"
]);
let ExcludedPatterns = dynamic(["terraform", "bicep", "pipeline", "arm-", "policy-compliance"]);
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
| where OperationNameValue has_any (HighImpactOps)
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| where not(tolower(Caller) has_any (ExcludedPatterns))
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| where CallerIpAddress !startswith "168.63."
| summarize
    OperationCount = count(),
    Operations = make_set(OperationNameValue, 10),
    AffectedResources = make_set(ResourceId, 10),
    SourceIPs = make_set(CallerIpAddress, 5),
    CallerIP = any(CallerIpAddress),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Caller, SubscriptionId, ResourceGroup
| extend
    AccountName = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[0]),
    AccountUPNSuffix = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[1])

resources.comments: RULE-08 | SP Credential Added Then Privileged Role Assigned by Same Initiator | T1098.001 | High
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/b8c9d0e1-f2a3-4567-bcde-678901234567')]

properties.displayName: Azure - Service Principal Credential Added Then Privileged Role Assigned (Same Initiator, 60 min)
properties.description: Detects when the same identity first adds a credential to a service principal and then assigns it (or another SP) a privileged role — both within 60 minutes. This is the Azure-native 'create backdoor SP' attack chain (T1098.001) used by groups like Storm-0501 and Midnight Blizzard to establish persistent privileged access. NOT a duplicate of built-in 'First access credential added to Application' rule which only covers the first credential add without the role-escalation correlation. FP mitigations: (1) Role name filtering — only Owner, Contributor, UAA, Global Admin, Privileged Role Admin trigger; (2) 60-minute correlation window keeps false positives low while catching fast-moving attacks; (3) Requires both events to share the same Initiator identity.
properties.query:
let CredentialAdditions = AuditLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
    | where OperationName has_any ("Add service principal credentials", "Update application – Certificates and secrets management")
    | where Result =~ "success"
    | extend SPName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
    | extend Initiator = coalesce(tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName), tostring(InitiatedBy.app.displayName))
    | where isnotempty(Initiator)
    | project CredTime = TimeGenerated, Initiator, SPName, CorrelationId;
let PrivilegedRoleAssignments = AuditLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
    | where OperationName has_any ("Add member to role", "Add app role assignment to service principal", "Add eligible member to role")
    | where Result =~ "success"
    | extend RoleName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
    | where RoleName has_any ("Owner", "Contributor", "User Access Administrator", "Global Administrator", "Privileged Role Administrator", "Security Administrator", "Application Administrator")
    | extend Initiator = coalesce(tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName), tostring(InitiatedBy.app.displayName))
    | where isnotempty(Initiator)
    | project RoleTime = TimeGenerated, Initiator, RoleName, CorrelationId;
CredentialAdditions
| join kind=inner PrivilegedRoleAssignments on Initiator
| where RoleTime >= CredTime and RoleTime <= CredTime + 1h
| summarize
    EventCount = count(),
    AssignedRoles = make_set(RoleName, 5),
    AffectedSPs = make_set(SPName, 5),
    CorrelationIds = make_set(CorrelationId, 5),
    FirstEvent = min(CredTime),
    LastEvent = max(RoleTime)
    by Initiator
| extend
    AccountName = tostring(split(Initiator, "@")[0]),
    AccountUPNSuffix = tostring(split(Initiator, "@")[1])

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: Backdoor SP: {{Initiator}} added credential then assigned {{AssignedRoles}}
alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: {{Initiator}} added credentials to SP(s) {{AffectedSPs}} and subsequently assigned privileged role(s) {{AssignedRoles}} within 60 minutes.

resources.comments: RULE-09 | Privileged Azure Management Operations from a New IP for Established Identity | T1078.004 | Medium
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/c9d0e1f2-a3b4-5678-cdef-789012345678')]

properties.displayName: Azure - Privileged Management Operations from a New Source IP for Established Identity
properties.description: Detects when a known identity (seen in the last 14 days) performs privileged Azure management operations from an IP address not previously used by that identity for such operations. This is the Azure-plane equivalent of Impossible Travel / anomalous access location, but for the management plane specifically. Relevant to T1078.004 (Valid Cloud Accounts) exploitation — compromised credentials used from attacker infrastructure. FP mitigations: (1) 14-day per-identity IP whitelist built from historical privileged ops; (2) Azure platform IPs excluded; (3) Uses set_has_element for exact match — no partial-IP tricks; (4) Only privileged operation types (write/delete on IAM, KV, VM, diagnostics, automation); (5) Identity must have prior history (new identities with no baseline are a different rule).
properties.query:
let PrivilegedOps = dynamic([
    "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEASSIGNMENTS/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEASSIGNMENTS/DELETE",
    "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/DELETE",
    "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.INSIGHTS/DIAGNOSTICSETTINGS/DELETE",
    "MICROSOFT.AUTOMATION/AUTOMATIONACCOUNTS/RUNBOOKS/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/LOCKS/DELETE"
]);
let HistoricalCallerIPs = AzureActivity
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(14d) .. ago(6h))
    | where OperationNameValue has_any (PrivilegedOps)
    | where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
    | where isnotempty(Caller) and isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
    | summarize HistoricalIPs = make_set(CallerIpAddress, 50) by Caller;
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(6h)
| where OperationNameValue has_any (PrivilegedOps)
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| where CallerIpAddress !startswith "168.63." and CallerIpAddress !startswith "169.254."
| join kind=inner HistoricalCallerIPs on Caller
| where not(set_has_element(HistoricalIPs, CallerIpAddress))
| summarize
    OperationCount = count(),
    Operations = make_set(OperationNameValue, 10),
    AffectedResources = make_set(ResourceId, 10),
    CallerIP = any(CallerIpAddress),
    SourceIPs = make_set(CallerIpAddress, 5),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Caller, SubscriptionId
| extend
    AccountName = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[0]),
    AccountUPNSuffix = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[1])

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: New IP for {{Caller}}: privileged Azure ops from {{CallerIP}}
alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: Established identity {{Caller}} performed {{OperationCount}} privileged management operation(s) from a new IP {{CallerIP}} not seen in 14 days.

resources.comments: RULE-10 | Service Principal Signed In with CA Bypass Then Performed Azure Management Ops | T1528 | High
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/d0e1f2a3-b4c5-6789-defa-890123456789')]

properties.displayName: Azure - Service Principal CA Bypass Sign-in Followed by Management Operations
properties.description: Detects when a service principal sign-in has Conditional Access status 'failure' or 'notApplied' (CA bypass) but ResultType=0 (sign-in succeeded), and that same source IP subsequently calls Azure management APIs within 1 hour. This is indicative of stolen SP tokens or credential abuse where the attacker bypassed CA policies (T1528 — Steal Application Access Token). Correlates AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs with AzureActivity on IP. FP mitigations: (1) ResultType=0 required — prevents alerting on simply failed sign-ins; (2) Requires 2+ management operations post sign-in — filters out single read operations; (3) Azure platform and link-local IPs excluded; (4) 1-hour temporal window keeps the correlation tight and reduces coincidental IP matches from shared NATs.
properties.query:
let CABypassSignins = AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
    | where ResultType == 0
    | where ConditionalAccessStatus has_any ("failure", "notApplied")
    | where isnotempty(IPAddress)
    | where IPAddress !startswith "168.63." and IPAddress !startswith "169.254."
    | project SigninTime = TimeGenerated, ServicePrincipalId, ServicePrincipalName, SigninIP = IPAddress, AppId, ConditionalAccessStatus;
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| where OperationNameValue has_any ("WRITE", "DELETE", "ACTION")
| where not(OperationNameValue has_any ("READ", "LIST", "GET", "LISTKEYS"))
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| join kind=inner CABypassSignins on $left.CallerIpAddress == $right.SigninIP
| where TimeGenerated > SigninTime and TimeGenerated <= SigninTime + 1h
| summarize
    MGMTOpCount = count(),
    Operations = make_set(OperationNameValue, 10),
    AffectedResources = make_set(ResourceId, 10),
    SubscriptionIds = make_set(SubscriptionId, 5),
    CallerIP = any(CallerIpAddress),
    CAStatus = any(ConditionalAccessStatus),
    FirstMGMTOp = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastMGMTOp = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Caller, ServicePrincipalName, SigninTime
| where MGMTOpCount >= 2
| extend
    AccountName = coalesce(tostring(split(Caller, "@")[0]), ServicePrincipalName),
    AccountUPNSuffix = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[1])

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: CA bypass SP {{ServicePrincipalName}} then {{MGMTOpCount}} MGMT ops from {{CallerIP}}
alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: SP {{ServicePrincipalName}} signed in (CA bypass) from {{CallerIP}}, then performed {{MGMTOpCount}} management operations within 1 hour.

resources.comments: HUNT-01 | GPU and High-Compute VM Deployment History (90 days)
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/hunt-gpu-vm-deployments-90d')]

properties.displayName: Hunt - GPU and High-Compute VM Deployment History (90d)
properties.query:
let GPUSKUs = dynamic(["Standard_NC", "Standard_NV", "Standard_ND", "Standard_NP", "Standard_HB", "Standard_HC"]);
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
| where OperationNameValue has_any ("VIRTUALMACHINES/WRITE", "VIRTUALMACHINESCALESETS/WRITE")
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| where Properties has_any (GPUSKUs)
| project TimeGenerated, Caller, CallerIpAddress, SubscriptionId, ResourceGroup, ResourceId, Properties
| extend SKUHint = extract(@'Standard_(?:NC|NV|ND|NP|HB|HC)[^"]*', 0, Properties)
| order by TimeGenerated desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Inventory all successful GPU/high-compute VM deployments over the past 90 days. Use to baseline legitimate deployers before tuning Rule-01, and to investigate potential cryptojacking campaigns.

resources.comments: HUNT-02 | Diagnostic Settings Change Timeline per Resource
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/hunt-diagnostic-settings-timeline')]

properties.displayName: Hunt - Diagnostic Settings Change Timeline per Resource (90d)
properties.query:
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
| where OperationNameValue has "DIAGNOSTICSETTINGS"
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| project TimeGenerated, Operation = OperationNameValue, Caller, ResourceId, CallerIpAddress, SubscriptionId, ResourceGroup
| extend IsDelete = Operation has "DELETE"
| order by ResourceId asc, TimeGenerated asc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Show the full create/update/delete history of diagnostic settings per resource. Use to identify resources that were permanently unmonitored by correlating delete events with no subsequent write.

resources.comments: HUNT-03 | Storage Blob High-Volume Read by Single IP (Potential Exfiltration)
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/hunt-storage-blob-exfiltration')]

properties.displayName: Hunt - Storage Blob High-Volume Read by Single IP (Potential Exfiltration)
properties.query:
StorageBlobLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName == "GetBlob"
| where StatusCode == "200"
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| where CallerIpAddress !startswith "168.63."
| summarize
    ReadCount = count(),
    TotalBytesGB = round(sum(ResponseBodySize) / 1073741824.0, 2),
    DistinctBlobs = dcount(ObjectKey),
    StorageAccounts = make_set(AccountName, 5),
    EarliestRead = min(TimeGenerated),
    LatestRead = max(TimeGenerated)
    by CallerIpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where ReadCount > 500 or TotalBytesGB > 1.0
| order by TotalBytesGB desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Identify IPs that performed high-volume blob reads (>500 reads/hr or >1 GB/hr). Correlate with SAS token generation (Rule-04) to confirm exfiltration via ephemeral SAS credentials.

resources.comments: HUNT-04 | Azure Automation Runbook Create/Modify/Publish History
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/hunt-automation-runbook-history')]

properties.displayName: Hunt - Azure Automation Runbook Create, Modify, Publish History (90d)
properties.query:
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
| where OperationNameValue has "AUTOMATION"
| where OperationNameValue has_any ("RUNBOOKS", "WEBHOOKS", "SCHEDULES", "VARIABLES", "MODULES")
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| project TimeGenerated, Caller, Operation = OperationNameValue, ResourceName = tostring(split(ResourceId, "/")[-1]), ResourceId, CallerIpAddress, ResourceGroup, SubscriptionId
| order by TimeGenerated desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Full history of Automation Account object changes. Use to identify newly created runbooks, unexpected content changes, and newly added webhooks (used for C2 callbacks). Correlate Caller with Rule-05 first-time-caller alerts.

resources.comments: HUNT-05 | Threat Intelligence Indicators Matched in AzureActivity Management Plane (Extended 90d)
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/hunt-ti-mgmt-plane-extended')]

properties.displayName: Hunt - TI Indicator Match in Azure Management Plane — Extended 90 Days
properties.query:
let PrivateRanges = dynamic(["10.", "192.168.", "172.16.", "172.17.", "172.18.", "172.19.", "172.20.", "172.21.", "172.22.", "172.23.", "172.24.", "172.25.", "172.26.", "172.27.", "172.28.", "172.29.", "172.30.", "172.31.", "127.", "169.254.", "168.63."]);
let TIIPIndicators = ThreatIntelIndicators
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
    | where Confidence >= 50
    | where ObservableKey =~ "ipv4-addr:value"
    | where isnotempty(ObservableValue)
    | where not(ObservableValue has_any (PrivateRanges))
    | summarize
        ThreatType     = any(tostring(Data.indicator_types)),
        TI_Description = any(tostring(Data.description)),
        ConfScore      = max(Confidence),
        ActivityGroups = any(Tags),
        TLPLevel       = any(tostring(AdditionalFields.TLPLevel)),
        IsActive       = any(IsActive),
        Revoked        = any(Revoked)
        by TI_IP = ObservableValue;
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| where not(CallerIpAddress has_any (PrivateRanges))
| join kind=inner TIIPIndicators on $left.CallerIpAddress == $right.TI_IP
| project TimeGenerated, Caller, CallerIpAddress, OperationNameValue, ActivityStatusValue, ResourceGroup, SubscriptionId, TI_IP, ThreatType, TI_Description, ConfScore, ActivityGroups, TLPLevel, IsActive, Revoked
| order by TimeGenerated desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Extended 90-day TI correlation with lower confidence threshold (>=50) for hunting. Includes inactive/expired indicators. Use to find historical compromise before TI feeds were ingested or indicators went active.

resources.comments: HUNT-06 | Privileged Role Assignments to Disabled or Deleted AAD Identities
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/hunt-orphaned-privileged-roles')]

properties.displayName: Hunt - Privileged Role Assignments Matching Disabled or Deleted AAD Identities
properties.query:
let DisabledUsers = AuditLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
    | where OperationName has_any ("Disable account", "Delete user", "Update user")
    | where Result =~ "success"
    | where OperationName !has "Enable"
    | extend TargetId = tostring(TargetResources[0].id), TargetUPN = tostring(TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName)
    | summarize DisabledAt = max(ActivityDateTime) by TargetId, TargetUPN;
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
| where OperationNameValue =~ "MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/ROLEASSIGNMENTS/WRITE"
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| extend AssignedPrincipalId = tostring(parse_json(Properties).requestbody.properties.principalId)
| extend RoleDefId = tostring(parse_json(Properties).requestbody.properties.roleDefinitionId)
| where isnotempty(AssignedPrincipalId)
| join kind=inner DisabledUsers on $left.AssignedPrincipalId == $right.TargetId
| where TimeGenerated > DisabledAt
| project TimeGenerated, Caller, AssignedPrincipalId, TargetUPN, RoleDefId, ResourceGroup, SubscriptionId, DisabledAt
| order by TimeGenerated desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Finds role assignments made AFTER the target identity was disabled or deleted. This indicates either an attacker re-using a known principal ID to maintain access, or a cleanup gap creating a dormant privilege.

resources.comments: HUNT-07 | Azure Lighthouse Delegation Changes
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/hunt-lighthouse-delegation-changes')]

properties.displayName: Hunt - Azure Lighthouse Managed Services Delegation Changes (90d)
properties.query:
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
| where OperationNameValue has "MICROSOFT.MANAGEDSERVICES"
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| extend OperationType = case(
    OperationNameValue has "DELETE", "Delegation Removed",
    OperationNameValue has "WRITE", "Delegation Added or Modified",
    "Other")
| project TimeGenerated, OperationType, Caller, Operation = OperationNameValue, ResourceId, CallerIpAddress, SubscriptionId
| order by TimeGenerated desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Shows all Azure Lighthouse (ManagedServices) delegation events. Attackers who gain Owner rights may create Lighthouse delegations to grant a foreign tenant persistent cross-subscription access that survives password resets.

resources.comments: HUNT-08 | Privileged Azure Operations Outside Business Hours (UTC)
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/hunt-privileged-ops-outside-hours')]

properties.displayName: Hunt - Privileged Azure Management Operations Outside Business Hours (UTC 07-19)
properties.query:
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| where OperationNameValue has_any (
    "ROLEASSIGNMENTS/WRITE",
    "ROLEASSIGNMENTS/DELETE",
    "VIRTUALMACHINES/WRITE",
    "DIAGNOSTICSETTINGS/DELETE",
    "KEYVAULTS/WRITE",
    "AUTOMATION/AUTOMATIONACCOUNTS/RUNBOOKS/WRITE",
    "LOCKS/DELETE",
    "POLICYASSIGNMENTS/DELETE")
| where isnotempty(Caller)
| extend HourUTC = hourofday(TimeGenerated)
| extend DayOfWeek = dayofweek(TimeGenerated)
| extend IsOffHours = HourUTC < 7 or HourUTC > 19 or DayOfWeek == 0d or DayOfWeek == 6d
| where IsOffHours == true
| summarize
    OffHoursOpCount = count(),
    Operations = make_set(OperationNameValue, 10),
    HoursActive = make_set(HourUTC, 24),
    SourceIPs = make_set(CallerIpAddress, 5)
    by Caller, SubscriptionId
| order by OffHoursOpCount desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Identifies identities performing sensitive management operations outside UTC 07:00-19:00 weekdays. Attackers in different time zones or running automated scripts often operate outside normal business hours. Review against expected schedules.

resources.comments: HUNT-09 | Service Principals Accessing Key Vault for the First Time
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/hunt-new-sp-keyvault-access')]

properties.displayName: Hunt - Service Principals Accessing Key Vault for the First Time Within 7 Days
properties.query:
let AllKVAccess = AzureActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
    | where OperationNameValue startswith "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/"
    | where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
    | summarize FirstAccessEver = min(TimeGenerated) by Caller, VaultPath = tostring(strcat(SubscriptionId, "/", ResourceGroup, "/", tostring(split(ResourceId, "/")[8])));
AllKVAccess
| where FirstAccessEver > ago(7d)
| join kind=inner (
    AzureActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where OperationNameValue startswith "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/"
    | where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
    | project TimeGenerated, Caller, Operation = OperationNameValue, ResourceId, CallerIpAddress, SubscriptionId, ResourceGroup
) on Caller
| project TimeGenerated, Caller, Operation, ResourceId, CallerIpAddress, SubscriptionId, ResourceGroup, FirstAccessEver
| order by FirstAccessEver desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Surfaces identities that accessed a Key Vault for the first time in 7 days. Compromised SPs often probe Key Vaults for secrets/certs after initial access. Investigate any SP not expected to require KV access.

resources.comments: HUNT-10 | Service Principal Credential Rotation Anomaly (Multiple Additions in 1-Hour Window)
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/hunt-sp-credential-rotation-anomaly')]

properties.displayName: Hunt - Service Principal Credential Rotation Anomaly (3+ Additions per Hour, 30 Days)
properties.query:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName has_any ("Add service principal credentials", "Update application – Certificates and secrets management")
| where Result =~ "success"
| extend SPName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend SPId = tostring(TargetResources[0].id)
| extend Initiator = coalesce(tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName), tostring(InitiatedBy.app.displayName))
| summarize
    CredAddCount = count(),
    AffectedSPs = make_set(SPName, 10),
    SPIds = make_set(SPId, 10),
    Initiators = make_set(Initiator, 5),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Initiator, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where CredAddCount >= 3
| order by CredAddCount desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Detects 3+ SP credentials added in 1 hour by the same initiator. Used for persistence sweeps or credential stuffing across SP objects. Cross-reference with Rule-08 (SP Cred + Privileged Role).

resources.comments: RULE-11 | Mass Resource Deletion - NSGs/VMs/Storage/VNETs/KeyVaults | T1485/T1562 | High
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/e1f2a3b4-c5d6-7890-efab-901234567890')]

properties.displayName: Azure - Mass Deletion of Critical Resources (NSGs/VMs/Storage/VNETs/Key Vaults)
properties.description: Detects bulk deletion of critical Azure infrastructure resources (NSGs, VMs, Storage Accounts, VNETs, Disks, Key Vaults, Subnets) by a single identity within a 1-hour window. This pattern is characteristic of ransomware pre-staging (remove defenses before encrypting), destructive insider attacks (T1485 — Data Destruction), and adversary cleanup after cloud-environment abuse (T1562). Correlates with SigninLogs and AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs to surface whether the deleting identity had a suspicious preceding sign-in (risky, from TOR, CA failure, or first-time location). FP mitigations: (1) Minimum 10 distinct resource deletions — filters normal day-to-day single resource removals; (2) IaC/pipeline caller patterns excluded — Terraform destroy, Bicep, GitHub Actions deprovision legitimately; (3) Azure platform IPs excluded; (4) 14-day baseline excludes identities with a pattern of high-volume deletions; (5) Requires at least 2 distinct resource types — prevents alert on repeated deletion of same type (e.g., cleaning up test VMs of same kind). NOT a duplicate of any confirmed live built-in alert (verified against live SecurityAlert data).
properties.query:
let LookupWindow = 1h;
let DeleteThreshold = 10;
let ResourceTypeThreshold = 2;
let KnownIaCPatterns = dynamic(["terraform", "bicep", "pipeline", "github", "pulumi", "devops", "arm-deployment", "cleanup", "deprovisioner"]);
let AzurePlatformIPs = dynamic(["168.63.", "169.254."]);
// Identities with established high-volume deletion patterns (14-day baseline)
let KnownBulkDeleters = AzureActivity
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(14d) .. ago(2h))
    | where OperationNameValue has "DELETE"
    | where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
    | summarize DailyDeletes = count() by Caller, Day = bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
    | summarize AvgDailyDeletes = avg(DailyDeletes), DaysActive = dcount(Day) by Caller
    | where AvgDailyDeletes >= 10 and DaysActive >= 5
    | distinct Caller;
// Critical resource DELETE operations (last 2h to capture full 1h windows)
let CriticalDeletes = AzureActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
    | where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
    | where OperationNameValue has "DELETE"
    | where OperationNameValue has_any (
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS",
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES",
        "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/VIRTUALNETWORKS",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/SUBNETS",
        "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/DISKS",
        "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/APPLICATIONGATEWAYS",
        "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/LOADBALANCERS"
    )
    | where not(tolower(Caller) has_any (KnownIaCPatterns))
    | where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
    | where not(CallerIpAddress has_any (AzurePlatformIPs))
    | where Caller !in (KnownBulkDeleters);
// Aggregate per caller/IP in 1-hour tumbling windows
let DeleteBursts = CriticalDeletes
    | summarize
        DeleteCount = count(),
        DistinctResourceTypes = dcount(tostring(split(OperationNameValue, "/")[1])),
        DeletedResources = make_set(ResourceId, 20),
        Operations = make_set(OperationNameValue, 10),
        ResourceGroups = make_set(ResourceGroup, 5),
        SubscriptionIds = make_set(SubscriptionId, 5),
        CallerIP = any(CallerIpAddress),
        SourceIPs = make_set(CallerIpAddress, 5),
        FirstDelete = min(TimeGenerated),
        LastDelete = max(TimeGenerated)
        by Caller, bin(TimeGenerated, LookupWindow)
    | where DeleteCount >= DeleteThreshold
    | where DistinctResourceTypes >= ResourceTypeThreshold;
// Enrich: correlate with SigninLogs for suspicious human sign-ins
let UserSigninContext = SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(3h)
    | where isnotempty(IPAddress)
    | project
        SigninTime = TimeGenerated,
        UserPrincipalName,
        SigninIP = IPAddress,
        UserDisplayName,
        RiskLevelAggregated,
        RiskState,
        ConditionalAccessStatus,
        Location,
        AppDisplayName
    | extend IsRisky = RiskLevelAggregated in ("high", "medium") or RiskState =~ "atRisk";
// Enrich: correlate with SP sign-ins
let SPSigninContext = AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(3h)
    | where isnotempty(IPAddress)
    | project
        SPSigninTime = TimeGenerated,
        ServicePrincipalName,
        ServicePrincipalId,
        SPSigninIP = IPAddress,
        SPConditionalAccessStatus = ConditionalAccessStatus,
        SPResultType = ResultType;
DeleteBursts
| join kind=leftouter (
    UserSigninContext
    | summarize
        UserSigninCount = count(),
        SigninLocations = make_set(Location, 3),
        SigninApps = make_set(AppDisplayName, 3),
        MaxRisk = max(RiskLevelAggregated),
        HasRiskySignin = max(toint(IsRisky)),
        UserNames = make_set(UserPrincipalName, 3)
        by SigninIP
  ) on $left.CallerIP == $right.SigninIP
| join kind=leftouter (
    SPSigninContext
    | summarize
        SPSigninCount = count(),
        SigninSPs = make_set(ServicePrincipalName, 3),
        CABypassCount = countif(SPConditionalAccessStatus has_any ("failure", "notApplied"))
        by SPSigninIP
  ) on $left.CallerIP == $right.SPSigninIP
| extend
    HasSigninCorroboration = isnotempty(UserSigninCount) or isnotempty(SPSigninCount),
    SigninRiskLevel = coalesce(MaxRisk, "unknown"),
    AccountName = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[0]),
    AccountUPNSuffix = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[1])
| project-away SigninIP, SPSigninIP

alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: Identity {{Caller}} deleted {{DeleteCount}} critical resources. Sign-in risk: {{SigninRiskLevel}}.

resources.comments: RULE-12 | Mass Resource Creation Burst by Identity with No Prior Creation History | T1496/T1136 | High
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/f2a3b4c5-d6e7-8901-fabc-012345678901')]

properties.displayName: Azure - Mass Resource Creation Burst by New Identity (Cryptojacking / Persistence)
properties.description: Detects bursts of resource creation events by identities with no prior resource-creation history in the last 14 days, across 3+ distinct resource types, within a 2-hour window. This pattern is characteristic of: (1) Cryptojacking operators provisioning GPU/compute fleets post-credential-theft (T1496); (2) Attackers establishing persistence infrastructure — VMs, App Registrations, Container Instances (T1136); (3) Supply-chain-compromised pipelines spinning up outbound command-and-control infrastructure. Differentiated from the built-in 'Unusual number of resources created' alert because this rule uses IDENTITY-BASELINE logic (only triggers for identities with ZERO creation history) rather than simple count thresholds, and requires TYPE DIVERSITY (3+ distinct resource types) to signal broad infrastructure provisioning rather than legitimate scaling operations. Enriched with SigninLogs and AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs to surface whether the creating identity had a suspicious preceding authentication. FP mitigations: (1) 14-day identity creation baseline — established deployers never trigger; (2) 3+ distinct resource type requirement — avoids alert on auto-scaling of single resource type; (3) Minimum 15 resource events in 2h — accommodates IaC deployments that legitimately provision dozens of resources but filters single-test deployments; (4) IaC/CI-CD tool pattern exclusion; (5) Azure platform IP exclusion; (6) Only Success status operations.
properties.query:
let LookupWindow = 2h;
let CreationThreshold = 15;
let ResourceTypeThreshold = 3;
let KnownIaCPatterns = dynamic(["terraform", "bicep", "pipeline", "github", "pulumi", "devops", "arm-deployment", "provisioner", "deployer"]);
let AzurePlatformIPs = dynamic(["168.63.", "169.254."]);
// Resource WRITE operations we care about (creation/provisioning)
let CreationOpKeywords = dynamic([
    "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINESCALESETS/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.STORAGE/STORAGEACCOUNTS/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.WEB/SITES/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.CONTAINERINSTANCE/CONTAINERGROUPS/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.CONTAINERSERVICE/MANAGEDCLUSTERS/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.KEYVAULT/VAULTS/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/VIRTUALNETWORKS/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/DISKS/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.DATABRICKS/WORKSPACES/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.LOGIC/WORKFLOWS/WRITE"
]);
// 14-day baseline: identities with established resource creation history
let KnownCreators = AzureActivity
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(14d) .. ago(3h))
    | where OperationNameValue has_any (CreationOpKeywords)
    | where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
    | distinct Caller;
// Current window: creation bursts by UNKNOWN identities
let CreationBursts = AzureActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(3h)
    | where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
    | where OperationNameValue has_any (CreationOpKeywords)
    | where not(tolower(Caller) has_any (KnownIaCPatterns))
    | where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
    | where not(CallerIpAddress has_any (AzurePlatformIPs))
    | where Caller !in (KnownCreators)
    | summarize
        CreationCount = count(),
        DistinctResourceTypes = dcount(tostring(split(OperationNameValue, "/")[1])),
        CreatedResources = make_set(ResourceId, 20),
        Operations = make_set(OperationNameValue, 10),
        ResourceGroups = make_set(ResourceGroup, 5),
        SubscriptionIds = make_set(SubscriptionId, 5),
        CallerIP = any(CallerIpAddress),
        SourceIPs = make_set(CallerIpAddress, 5),
        FirstCreate = min(TimeGenerated),
        LastCreate = max(TimeGenerated)
        by Caller, bin(TimeGenerated, LookupWindow)
    | where CreationCount >= CreationThreshold
    | where DistinctResourceTypes >= ResourceTypeThreshold;
// Enrich: correlate with user sign-ins from same IP
let UserSigninContext = SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(4h)
    | where isnotempty(IPAddress)
    | project
        SigninTime = TimeGenerated,
        UserPrincipalName,
        SigninIP = IPAddress,
        UserDisplayName,
        RiskLevelAggregated,
        RiskState,
        ConditionalAccessStatus,
        Location,
        AppDisplayName,
        IsInteractive
    | extend IsRisky = RiskLevelAggregated in ("high", "medium") or RiskState =~ "atRisk";
// Enrich: correlate with SP sign-ins from same IP
let SPSigninContext = AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(4h)
    | where isnotempty(IPAddress)
    | project
        SPSigninTime = TimeGenerated,
        ServicePrincipalName,
        ServicePrincipalId,
        SPSigninIP = IPAddress,
        SPConditionalAccessStatus = ConditionalAccessStatus,
        SPResultType = ResultType;
CreationBursts
| join kind=leftouter (
    UserSigninContext
    | summarize
        UserSigninCount = count(),
        SigninLocations = make_set(Location, 3),
        SigninApps = make_set(AppDisplayName, 3),
        MaxRisk = max(RiskLevelAggregated),
        HasRiskySignin = max(toint(IsRisky)),
        UserNames = make_set(UserPrincipalName, 3),
        FirstSignin = min(SigninTime)
        by SigninIP
  ) on $left.CallerIP == $right.SigninIP
| join kind=leftouter (
    SPSigninContext
    | summarize
        SPSigninCount = count(),
        SigninSPs = make_set(ServicePrincipalName, 3),
        CABypassCount = countif(SPConditionalAccessStatus has_any ("failure", "notApplied"))
        by SPSigninIP
  ) on $left.CallerIP == $right.SPSigninIP
| extend
    HasSigninCorroboration = isnotempty(UserSigninCount) or isnotempty(SPSigninCount),
    SigninRiskLevel = coalesce(MaxRisk, "unknown"),
    AccountName = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[0]),
    AccountUPNSuffix = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[1])
| project-away SigninIP, SPSigninIP

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: Mass creation: {{Caller}} created {{CreationCount}} resources ({{DistinctResourceTypes}} types) - first time seen
alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: New identity {{Caller}} created {{CreationCount}} resources in 2 hours from {{CallerIP}}. No prior creation history.

resources.comments: HUNT-11 | Storage Anonymous and SAS-Token Access Timeline | T1530 | Medium
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/hunt-storage-anonymous-sas-access-timeline')]

properties.displayName: Hunt - Storage Anonymous and SAS-Token Access Timeline (90 Days)
properties.query:
let PrivateRanges = dynamic(["10.", "172.16.", "172.17.", "172.18.", "172.19.", "172.20.", "172.21.", "172.22.", "172.23.", "172.24.", "172.25.", "172.26.", "172.27.", "172.28.", "172.29.", "172.30.", "172.31.", "192.168.", "168.63.", "169.254."]);
StorageBlobLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
| where AuthenticationType in~ ("Anonymous", "SasToken")
| where StatusCode == 200
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| where not(CallerIpAddress has_any (PrivateRanges))

resources.comments: HUNT-12 | NSG Deletion and Firewall Rule Modification History | T1562.007 | Medium
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/hunt-nsg-firewall-change-history')]

properties.displayName: Hunt - NSG Deletion and Firewall Rule Modification History (30 Days)
properties.query:
let NSGOps = dynamic([
    "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/DELETE",
    "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/SECURITYRULES/WRITE",

]);
AzureActivity

| extend ChangeType = case(
    OperationNameValue has "DELETE" and OperationNameValue has "NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/DELETE", "NSG Deleted",
    OperationNameValue has "SECURITYRULES/DELETE", "Security Rule Deleted",
    OperationNameValue has "SECURITYRULES/WRITE",  "Security Rule Modified/Added",
    OperationNameValue has "NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/WRITE", "NSG Created/Modified",

| project TimeGenerated, Caller, CallerIpAddress, ChangeType, ResourceId, ResourceGroup, SubscriptionId, Properties

tags.name: description

resources.comments: HUNT-13 | VM Script Extension and Run Command Execution History | T1059/T1059.009 | High
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/hunt-vm-script-extension-history')]

properties.displayName: Hunt - VM Custom Script Extension and Run Command History (30 Days)
properties.query:
let ScriptExtTypes = dynamic(["CustomScriptExtension", "CustomScriptForLinux", "RunCommandHandler", "RunCommand", "Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension", "Microsoft.Azure.Extensions.CustomScript"]);
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationNameValue =~ "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/EXTENSIONS/WRITE"
    or  OperationNameValue =~ "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/RUNCOMMAND/ACTION"
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| where Properties has_any (ScriptExtTypes) or OperationNameValue has "RUNCOMMAND"
| extend PropsParsed   = todynamic(Properties)
| extend ExtensionType = coalesce(tostring(PropsParsed["type"]), tostring(PropsParsed.type), iff(OperationNameValue has "RUNCOMMAND", "RunCommand", "Unknown"))
| extend VMName = tostring(split(ResourceId, "/")[8])
| summarize
    ExecCount     = count(),
    DistinctVMs   = dcount(VMName),
    VMNames       = make_set(VMName, 20),
    ExtensionTypes = make_set(ExtensionType, 5),
    SourceIPs     = make_set(CallerIpAddress, 5),
    FirstSeen     = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen      = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Caller, ResourceGroup, SubscriptionId
| order by ExecCount desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: All Custom Script Extension deployments and Run Command executions across VMs in 30 days. Identifies unusual actors, one-off script injections, or scripts outside known IaC patterns. Cross-reference with Rule-15 alerts.

resources.comments: HUNT-14 | Sentinel Configuration Changes (Rules, Connectors, Workbooks) | T1562 | High
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/hunt-sentinel-config-changes')]

properties.displayName: Hunt - Sentinel Configuration Changes (Rules, Connectors, Automation) (30 Days)
properties.query:
let SentinelOps = dynamic([
    "MICROSOFT.SECURITYINSIGHTS/ALERTRULES/DELETE",
    "MICROSOFT.SECURITYINSIGHTS/ALERTRULES/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.SECURITYINSIGHTS/DATACONNECTORS/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.SECURITYINSIGHTS/DATACONNECTORS/DELETE",
    "MICROSOFT.SECURITYINSIGHTS/AUTOMATIONRULES/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.SECURITYINSIGHTS/AUTOMATIONRULES/DELETE",
    "MICROSOFT.SECURITYINSIGHTS/SETTINGS/WRITE",
    "MICROSOFT.SECURITYINSIGHTS/WORKSPACEMANAGERCONFIGURATIONS/WRITE"
]);
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationNameValue has_any (SentinelOps)
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| extend ChangeCategory = case(
    OperationNameValue has "ALERTRULES"      , "Analytics Rule",
    OperationNameValue has "DATACONNECTORS"  , "Data Connector",
    OperationNameValue has "AUTOMATIONRULES" , "Automation Rule",
    OperationNameValue has "SETTINGS"        , "Sentinel Settings",
    "Other")
| extend ActionType = iff(OperationNameValue has "DELETE", "Delete", "Create/Modify")
| project TimeGenerated, Caller, CallerIpAddress, ChangeCategory, ActionType, OperationNameValue, ResourceId, ResourceGroup, SubscriptionId
| order by TimeGenerated desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Full 30-day timeline of Sentinel configuration changes: alert rule deletions/modifications, data connector changes, automation rule changes, and workspace settings. Use to investigate Rule-16 alerts and audit who changed Sentinel during an incident.

resources.comments: HUNT-15 | Identity Operation Volume Anomaly Deep-Dive | T1078/T1580 | Medium
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/hunt-identity-ops-anomaly-deepdive')]

properties.displayName: Hunt - Identity Operation Volume Anomaly Deep-Dive (30 Days)
properties.query:
let ExcludedPatterns = dynamic(["terraform", "bicep", "pipeline", "github", "pulumi", "devops", "arm-deployment"]);
let AzurePlatformIPs = dynamic(["168.63.", "169.254."]);
let DailyOps = AzureActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
    | where OperationNameValue has_any ("WRITE", "DELETE", "ACTION")
    | where not(OperationNameValue has_any ("READ", "LIST", "GET", "LISTKEYS"))
    | where isnotempty(Caller) and isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
    | where not(CallerIpAddress has_any (AzurePlatformIPs))
    | where not(tolower(Caller) has_any (ExcludedPatterns))
    | summarize DailyCount = count() by Caller, Day = bin(TimeGenerated, 1d);
let Baseline = DailyOps
    | where Day < ago(7d)
    | summarize AvgDailyOps = avg(DailyCount), StdDevOps = stdev(DailyCount), SampleDays = dcount(Day) by Caller
    | where SampleDays >= 3;
let RecentOps = DailyOps | where Day >= ago(7d);
RecentOps
| join kind=inner Baseline on Caller
| extend DeviationScore = iff(StdDevOps > 0, (DailyCount - AvgDailyOps) / StdDevOps, toreal(DailyCount))
| where DeviationScore >= 2.5 or (AvgDailyOps < 1 and DailyCount >= 10)
| project Day, Caller, DailyCount, AvgDailyOps = round(AvgDailyOps, 1), StdDevOps = round(StdDevOps, 1), DeviationScore = round(DeviationScore, 1)
| order by DeviationScore desc, DailyCount desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Per-identity daily management op counts vs. 23-day baseline. Flags days exceeding 2.5 sigma above mean. Use to investigate Rule-17 alerts, find recon bursts, or surface compromised accounts.

resources.comments: RULE-13 | Storage Account Accessed Anonymously or via SAS from Public Internet | T1530 | Medium
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/f3a4b5c6-d7e8-9012-fabc-def123456789')]

properties.displayName: Azure - Storage Account Accessed Anonymously or via SAS from Public Internet
properties.description: Detects blob reads from public IPs using Anonymous or SAS-Token authentication. Anonymous access to Storage Blobs is disabled by default in new accounts but may still exist in legacy deployments; SAS tokens are frequently used by attackers post-compromise to exfiltrate data without AAD traces (T1530). FP mitigations: (1) Private IP ranges and Azure platform IPs excluded; (2) Anonymous access triggers at 5+ requests — filters one-off misconfiguration probes; (3) SAS threshold set at 50 MB to focus on meaningful data movement; (4) RequestStatus == 200 only — rejects failed/probing attempts.
properties.query:
let PrivateRanges = dynamic(["10.", "172.16.", "172.17.", "172.18.", "172.19.", "172.20.", "172.21.", "172.22.", "172.23.", "172.24.", "172.25.", "172.26.", "172.27.", "172.28.", "172.29.", "172.30.", "172.31.", "192.168.", "168.63.", "169.254."]);
let SensitiveOps = dynamic(["GetBlob", "ListBlobs", "GetBlobProperties", "GetContainerProperties", "ListContainers", "GetContainerACL"]);
let AnonAccessThreshold = 5;
let SASBytesGBThreshold = 0.05;
StorageBlobLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
| where AuthenticationType in~ ("Anonymous", "SasToken")
| where OperationName in (SensitiveOps)
| where StatusCode == 200
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| where not(CallerIpAddress has_any (PrivateRanges))
| summarize
    AccessCount      = count(),
    DistinctBlobs    = dcount(ObjectKey),
    DistinctContainers = dcount(tostring(split(ObjectKey, "/")[0])),
    TotalBytesGB     = round(sum(ResponseBodySize) / 1073741824.0, 3),
    Operations       = make_set(OperationName, 5),
    StorageAccounts  = make_set(AccountName, 5),
    FirstAccess      = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAccess       = max(TimeGenerated)
    by CallerIpAddress, AuthenticationType
| where (AuthenticationType =~ "Anonymous" and AccessCount >= AnonAccessThreshold)
    or  (AuthenticationType =~ "SasToken"  and TotalBytesGB >= SASBytesGBThreshold)
| extend
    CallerIP   = CallerIpAddress,
    IsAnonymous = AuthenticationType =~ "Anonymous"

alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: Public IP {{CallerIP}} accessed {{DistinctBlobs}} blob(s) using {{AuthenticationType}} authentication.

resources.comments: RULE-14 | Network Security Group Deleted | T1562.007 | High
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/a4b5c6d7-e8f9-0123-abcd-ef4567890123')]

properties.displayName: Azure - Network Security Group Deleted
properties.description: Detects successful deletion of any Network Security Group (NSG). NSG deletion removes perimeter firewall rules, exposing VMs and subnets to unrestricted traffic — a deliberate adversary tactic before lateral movement or data exfiltration (T1562.007 — Disable or Modify Cloud Firewall). This is NOT a duplicate of Rule-07 (Resource Lock/Policy Deleted) which targets governance controls, nor of Rule-11 (Mass Deletion) which requires 10+ resources across multiple types. This rule fires on any single NSG deletion by a non-IaC caller. FP mitigations: (1) Known IaC/pipeline caller patterns excluded; (2) Azure platform IPs excluded; (3) Success-only events.
properties.query:
let KnownIaCPatterns = dynamic(["terraform", "bicep", "pipeline", "github", "pulumi", "devops", "arm-deployment", "cleanup", "deprovisioner"]);
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
| where OperationNameValue =~ "MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/DELETE"
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| where not(tolower(Caller) has_any (KnownIaCPatterns))
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| where CallerIpAddress !startswith "168.63." and CallerIpAddress !startswith "169.254."
| summarize
    DeletionCount         = count(),
    DeletedNSGs           = make_set(ResourceId, 20),
    AffectedSubscriptions = make_set(SubscriptionId, 5),
    AffectedResourceGroups = make_set(ResourceGroup, 5),
    SourceIPs             = make_set(CallerIpAddress, 5),
    CallerIP              = any(CallerIpAddress),
    FirstSeen             = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen              = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Caller
| extend
    AccountName      = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[0]),
    AccountUPNSuffix = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[1])

resources.comments: RULE-15 | Custom Script Extension or Run Command Executed on VM | T1059/T1059.009 | High
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/b5c6d7e8-f9a0-1234-bcde-f56789012345')]

properties.displayName: Azure - Custom Script Extension or Run Command Executed on Virtual Machine
properties.description: Detects deployment of a Custom Script Extension (Windows or Linux) or execution of Run Command on an Azure VM by a non-IaC identity. Both mechanisms execute arbitrary code inside the VM with SYSTEM/root-level permissions — a favored persistence and lateral-movement technique (T1059.009 — Cloud Administration Command). Attackers use CustomScriptExtension to drop reverse shells, install crypto miners, or stage ransomware without touching the VM's OS login. FP mitigations: (1) Known IaC and config-management tool patterns excluded (Terraform, Ansible, Chef, Puppet, DSC, pipelines); (2) Azure platform IPs excluded; (3) Only 'Success' events — 'Start' events fired before execution is confirmed are excluded.
properties.query:
let KnownIaCPatterns = dynamic(["terraform", "bicep", "pipeline", "github", "pulumi", "devops", "arm-deployment", "chef", "puppet", "ansible", "dsc"]);
let ScriptExtTypes = dynamic(["CustomScriptExtension", "CustomScriptForLinux", "RunCommandHandler", "RunCommand", "Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension", "Microsoft.Azure.Extensions.CustomScript"]);
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
| where OperationNameValue =~ "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/EXTENSIONS/WRITE"
    or  OperationNameValue =~ "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/RUNCOMMAND/ACTION"
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| where Properties has_any (ScriptExtTypes) or OperationNameValue =~ "MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/RUNCOMMAND/ACTION"
| where not(tolower(Caller) has_any (KnownIaCPatterns))
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| where CallerIpAddress !startswith "168.63." and CallerIpAddress !startswith "169.254."
| extend PropsParsed    = todynamic(Properties)
| extend ExtensionType  = coalesce(tostring(PropsParsed["type"]), tostring(PropsParsed.type), iff(OperationNameValue has "RUNCOMMAND", "RunCommand", ""))
| extend VMName         = tostring(split(ResourceId, "/")[8])
| extend ExtensionName  = iff(array_length(split(ResourceId, "/")) > 10, tostring(split(ResourceId, "/")[10]), "")
| summarize
    ExecutionCount   = count(),
    AffectedVMs      = make_set(VMName, 10),
    ExtensionTypes   = make_set(ExtensionType, 5),
    AffectedResources = make_set(ResourceId, 10),
    SourceIPs        = make_set(CallerIpAddress, 5),
    CallerIP         = any(CallerIpAddress),
    FirstSeen        = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen         = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Caller, ResourceGroup, SubscriptionId
| extend
    AccountName      = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[0]),
    AccountUPNSuffix = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[1])

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: Script executed on VM(s) by {{Caller}} — {{ExtensionTypes}} on {{AffectedVMs}}

resources.comments: RULE-16 | Sentinel Analytics Rule Deleted or Disabled | T1562 | High
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/c6d7e8f9-a0b1-2345-cdef-678901234567')]

properties.displayName: Azure Sentinel - Analytics Rule Deleted or Disabled
properties.description: Detects deletion or bulk modification (disabling) of Microsoft Sentinel Analytics Rules. Attackers with sufficient Azure RBAC privileges delete or disable detection rules to blind the SIEM before conducting further activity (T1562 — Impair Defenses). This complements Rule-02 (Diagnostic Settings) by covering the Sentinel layer specifically. Alert fires on: (1) any single DELETE of an alert rule; (2) 3+ WRITE operations (bulk rule toggles/edits) from a non-IaC caller. FP mitigations: (1) IaC/pipeline patterns excluded — DevSecOps pipelines deploying rules via ARM/API are known-good; (2) Azure platform IPs excluded; (3) WRITE threshold of 3 to filter individual rule tuning.
properties.query:
let KnownIaCPatterns = dynamic(["terraform", "bicep", "pipeline", "github", "pulumi", "devops"]);
let SentinelRuleOps = dynamic(["MICROSOFT.SECURITYINSIGHTS/ALERTRULES/DELETE", "MICROSOFT.SECURITYINSIGHTS/ALERTRULES/WRITE"]);
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
| where OperationNameValue has_any (SentinelRuleOps)
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
| where not(tolower(Caller) has_any (KnownIaCPatterns))
| where isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
| where CallerIpAddress !startswith "168.63." and CallerIpAddress !startswith "169.254."
| extend OperationType = iff(OperationNameValue has "DELETE", "Delete", "Modify/Disable")
| summarize
    OperationCount     = count(),
    DeleteCount        = countif(OperationType == "Delete"),
    ModifyCount        = countif(OperationType == "Modify/Disable"),
    AffectedRules      = make_set(ResourceId, 20),
    Operations         = make_set(OperationNameValue, 5),
    AffectedWorkspaces = make_set(ResourceGroup, 5),
    SourceIPs          = make_set(CallerIpAddress, 5),
    CallerIP           = any(CallerIpAddress),
    FirstSeen          = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen           = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Caller, SubscriptionId
| where DeleteCount >= 1 or ModifyCount >= 3
| extend
    AccountName      = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[0]),
    AccountUPNSuffix = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[1])

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: Sentinel rule tampered by {{Caller}}: {{DeleteCount}} deleted, {{ModifyCount}} modified
alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: {{Caller}} deleted {{DeleteCount}} and modified {{ModifyCount}} Sentinel analytics rules.

resources.comments: RULE-17 | Anomalous Spike in Management Operations — Statistical Baseline | T1078/T1580 | Medium
resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/d7e8f9a0-b1c2-3456-defa-789012345678')]

properties.displayName: Azure - Anomalous Spike in Management Operations (Statistical Baseline Deviation)
properties.description: Detects identities whose current-hour write/delete/action operation count exceeds their personal 13-day hourly average by 3 or more standard deviations (and at least 20 ops in the current hour). This statistical approach catches compromised credentials performing bulk changes (ransomware staging, data wipe prep, or infrastructure abuse) without hard-wired thresholds that would miss high-volume accounts. Requires at least 3 days of baseline data per identity to avoid false positives on new accounts. FP mitigations: (1) IaC/pipeline callers excluded; (2) Azure platform IPs excluded; (3) Minimum 20 ops in current hour — filters statistical noise from low-volume accounts; (4) 3-sigma threshold yields <0.3% false positive rate on normal distributions; (5) Read/list/get operations excluded — write/delete/action only.
properties.query:
let DeviationThreshold = 3.0;
let MinOpsThreshold    = 20;
let MinBaselineDays    = 3;
let ExcludedPatterns   = dynamic(["terraform", "bicep", "pipeline", "github", "pulumi", "devops", "arm-deployment"]);
let AzurePlatformIPs   = dynamic(["168.63.", "169.254."]);
let Baseline = AzureActivity
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(14d) .. ago(1h))
    | where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
    | where OperationNameValue has_any ("WRITE", "DELETE", "ACTION")
    | where not(OperationNameValue has_any ("READ", "LIST", "GET", "LISTKEYS"))
    | where isnotempty(Caller) and isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
    | where not(CallerIpAddress has_any (AzurePlatformIPs))
    | where not(tolower(Caller) has_any (ExcludedPatterns))
    | summarize HourlyOps = count() by Caller, HourBin = bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
    | summarize AvgOpsPerHour = avg(HourlyOps), StdDevOps = stdev(HourlyOps), SampleDays = dcount(bin(HourBin, 1d)) by Caller
    | where SampleDays >= MinBaselineDays;
let CurrentOps = AzureActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
    | where ActivityStatusValue =~ "Success"
    | where OperationNameValue has_any ("WRITE", "DELETE", "ACTION")
    | where not(OperationNameValue has_any ("READ", "LIST", "GET", "LISTKEYS"))
    | where isnotempty(Caller) and isnotempty(CallerIpAddress)
    | where not(CallerIpAddress has_any (AzurePlatformIPs))
    | where not(tolower(Caller) has_any (ExcludedPatterns))
    | summarize
        CurrentHourOps   = count(),
        Operations       = make_set(OperationNameValue, 10),
        AffectedResources = make_set(ResourceId, 10),
        SourceIPs        = make_set(CallerIpAddress, 5),
        CallerIP         = any(CallerIpAddress),
        SubscriptionIds  = make_set(SubscriptionId, 5),
        ResourceGroups   = make_set(ResourceGroup, 5),
        FirstOp          = min(TimeGenerated),
        LastOp           = max(TimeGenerated)
        by Caller;
CurrentOps
| join kind=inner Baseline on Caller
| where CurrentHourOps >= MinOpsThreshold
| extend DeviationScore = iff(StdDevOps > 0, (CurrentHourOps - AvgOpsPerHour) / StdDevOps, toreal(CurrentHourOps))
| where DeviationScore >= DeviationThreshold or (AvgOpsPerHour < 1 and CurrentHourOps >= MinOpsThreshold)
| project Caller, CallerIP, SourceIPs, CurrentHourOps, AvgOpsPerHour = round(AvgOpsPerHour, 1), StdDevOps = round(StdDevOps, 1), DeviationScore = round(DeviationScore, 1), Operations, AffectedResources, SubscriptionIds, ResourceGroups, FirstOp, LastOp
| extend AccountName = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[0]), AccountUPNSuffix = tostring(split(Caller, "@")[1])

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: Anomalous ops: {{Caller}} — {{CurrentHourOps}} ops this hour ({{DeviationScore}}σ above baseline)
alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: {{Caller}} performed {{CurrentHourOps}} management operations in the last hour — {{DeviationScore}} standard deviations above baseline.
